title: Pending Corrections
description: Spec-silent shape (the approved build plan's Phase 2 section names 'data/pending_corrections.json' -- fed by audit.yml's findings or manual owner edits, drained first by the analyst each run -- without fixing fields; decision logged in IMPROVEMENT_BACKLOG.md). Top-level object: a version plus a queue of correction candidates awaiting the analyst's evidence check. A confirmed pending item becomes a content/corrections.json entry (schemas/corrections.schema.json) and its card's status is set to 'corrected'; an entry here is removed once actioned (not schema-tracked -- reconcile_run.py's own job). Phase 2 seeds data/pending_corrections.json = {"version": 1, "pending": []}. Phase 6 adds optional target_type/target_id, mirroring schemas/corrections.schema.json's own addition, so a pending candidate can eventually name a content/companies/<id>.json profile as well as a card -- card_id stays required for backward compatibility with today's card-only pipeline (data/pending_corrections.json's own pending[] is empty as of Phase 6, so this is a forward-looking addition, not a migration of real rows).

card_id.description: content/cards/<id>.json this candidate correction targets. Remains required for backward compatibility with the pipeline in place today, which only ever targets cards -- see target_type/target_id below for the forward-looking company-targeting hook.

target_type.description: Optional (Phase 6, forward-looking): what kind of record target_id names. Must be present together with target_id or not at all. Not yet populated by any current writer -- card_id above remains the field actually in use today.

target_id.description: Optional (Phase 6, forward-looking): the id of the record this candidate correction targets -- a content/cards/<id>.json id when target_type is 'card', or a content/companies/<id>.json id when target_type is 'company'. Must be present together with target_type or not at all.

issue_description.description: What might be wrong with the card, in plain language.

evidence_url.description: A source the analyst should fetch and weigh when checking this candidate.

source.description: Whether audit.yml's own checks (e.g. link rot, missed-story) or a manual owner edit raised this candidate.
